top_evidence: Full primitive equation ocean dynamics
KPP vertical mixing scheme
Sea ice thermodynamics and dynamics
Nonlinear free surface
Partial cell bottom topography for accurate bathymetry
MCL-1.level_description: Comprehensive process representation with validated interactions
MCL-1.override_reason: A stable, global multi-decadal ocean model requires sophisticated representation of salient ocean physical processes. MITgcm includes comprehensive ocean physics, KPP mixing, sea ice thermodynamics/dynamics, and optional biogeochemistry.

top_evidence: ECCO Version 4: 1 degree global configuration
LLC4320: 1/48 degree global ocean with tides (~2km)
Regional configurations down to O(100m) resolution
Lat-Lon-Cap grid enables efficient global coverage
One of ~3-4 codes capable of global O(100m) resolution
MCL-2.override_reason: ECCO/MITgcm operates across a wide range of spatial scales. The LLC4320 simulation demonstrates global submesoscale-resolving capability at 1/48 degree (~2km). ECCO products range from 1 degree to O(100m) resolution.

top_evidence: Hourly output from LLC4320 (tides, diurnal cycles)
Daily to monthly mean products
Multi-decadal continuous reanalysis (1992-present)
Consistent physics across all time scales
Sub-daily to decadal variability captured
MCL-3.level_description: Full spectrum with consistent physics across all scales
MCL-3.override_reason: ECCO products span hourly output to multi-decadal reanalysis (1992-present, 30+ years). The LLC4320 produces hourly output capturing tidal and sub-daily variability.

top_evidence: Ocean circulation (MITgcm core)
Sea ice model with thermodynamic and dynamic components
Ocean biogeochemistry (Darwin ecosystem model integration)
Thermodynamic ice sheet coupling for ice shelf/ocean interaction
MCL-4.override_reason: ECCO has 4 coupled components: ocean circulation, sea ice (thermodynamic+dynamic), biogeochemistry, and thermodynamic ice sheets. This is clearly HIGH (3-4 components). VERY_HIGH would require 5+ components or full ESM coupling.

top_evidence: ECCO produces retrospective ocean state estimates constrained by observations
High-resolution 'nature runs' are simulations, not forecasts
System is designed for historical reconstruction, not prediction
MCL-5.level_description: System is retrospective analysis, not designed for prediction
MCL-5.override_reason: ECCO is a retrospective state estimation / reanalysis system and high-resolution nature run facility. It is NOT a prediction system. Predictive skill is not applicable.

top_evidence: MITgcm is one of few codes capable of global O(100m) ocean resolution
Demonstrated scaling to hundreds of thousands of cores
LLC4320 simulation: 1/48 degree global ocean with tides
Adjoint model capability for large-scale optimization
MCL-6.override_reason: MITgcm (ECCO's underlying model) is one of only ~3-4 ocean model codes worldwide capable of running globally at O(100m) resolution. This represents world-class computational performance.

top_evidence: Satellite altimetry (Jason, Sentinel, etc.)
Sea surface temperature (multiple sources)
Sea surface salinity (Aquarius, SMOS, SMAP)
GRACE/GRACE-FO ocean bottom pressure
Argo float profiles (~2 million profiles)
Historical XBT, CTD, mooring data
Sea ice concentration and thickness
MCL-7.override_reason: ECCO's core mission is multi-source observational constraint. The system assimilates satellite altimetry, SST, SSS, GRACE gravity, Argo profiles, XBTs, CTDs, moorings, and more - approximately 10^9 observations.

top_evidence: ECCO Version 4 Release 4: 1992-2017 global ocean state estimate
ECCO Version 5: Extended and updated reanalysis
4D-Var adjoint method for optimal state estimation
Fully dynamically consistent solutions (no nudging artifacts)
Benchmarked against MERRA-2, ERA5, and other reanalyses
MCL-8.override_reason: Retrospective ocean state estimation IS ECCO's core mission. ECCO produces coupled, fully integrated, benchmarked reanalysis products spanning 1992-present (30+ years) using 4D-Var adjoint method.

top_evidence: Continuous validation against ~10^9 observations in state estimation
Formal cost function minimization quantifies model-data misfit
Participation in ocean model intercomparison projects (OMIP)
Systematic comparison with independent datasets not in assimilation
MCL-10.override_reason: ECCO involves continuous V&V against approximately 10^9 observations through its data assimilation framework. The optimization process IS verification/validation.

top_evidence: MITgcm is fully open source (MIT license) on GitHub
ECCO forcing fields publicly available
Analysis routines (ecco_v4_py) open source
Official releases CF/ACDD compliant per NASA ESDS mandate
Active user community and documentation
MCL-13.level_description: Fully open source, CF/ACDD compliant, active community
MCL-13.override_reason: ECCO model codes (MITgcm), forcing fields, and analysis routines are 100% open source. All official ECCO releases adhere to CF and ACDD conventions for interoperability, as mandated by NASA ESDS.
